title: Sesamoides minor
taxonomy: Sesamoides minor is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It belongs to the genus Sesamoides and is often classified as a member of the tribe Thelypodieae. Further classification places Sesamoides minor as a part of the subfamily Thelypodioideae. This plant is native to the southwestern regions of North America, specifically in the states of California, Nevada, and Utah. Its classification as a distinct species within the Brassicaceae family is supported by its unique morphological characteristics.

intro: Sesamoides minor, also known as the lesser sesame or rock samphire, is a perennial succulent plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to support digestive health and lower blood pressure. The bioactive constituents of Sesamoides minor include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
